articles.title: Tara Sutaria's vintage ina modern home speaks Victorian minimalism: Art inspirations to take away
articles.description: Tara Sutaria’s home feels like a living mood board: all things calm, warm, and quietly luxurious, with just enough detail to tell a story without being loud. In her Instagram post, she calls it “new beginnings,” “endless laughter and love,” and a space to welcome 2026 with open arms, and the images rightly echo that feeling.Her home adds just the touch of old-money vintage in minimalism. It is not busy or over‐styled; instead, it sits in a soft middle ground where art, colour, and texture gently invite you in. For anyone who loves interiors, there’s a lot to learn from how she uses objects not just as décor but as emotional anchors and subtle symbols of art.Here are some creative art inspirations that you can steal from Tara Sutaria’s home

articles.title: Check Out McFarlane Toys' New Mortal Kombat Klassic Sub-Zero And Reptile Figures
articles.description: Mortal Kombat is back in the spotlight thanks to the release of the new live-action film, and if you're looking to bring some of that video game magic home, you can check out two new collectible action figures. McFarlane Toys is expanding its line of Mortal Kombat toys with two new "Klassic" Sub-Zero and Reptile figures. Bearing a design that harkens back to their arcade roots, each figure is also constructed from translucent materials to make them stand out on your shelf and you can preorder them ahead of their August 23 arrival. More Mortal Kombat Klassic Figures To expand the collection--and host your very own Mortal Kombat tournament--you can pick up several more figures from McFarlane Toys. The lineup currently includes fan-favorite characters like Johnny Cage , Raiden , and Ermac alongside a regular version of Reptile. Ermac -- $30 Jade -- $35 Johnny Cage -- $32 Kitana -- $30 Noob Saibot -- $35 Raiden -- $29.51 Reptile -- $30 Reptile (Invisible) -- $32 Sub-Zero (Ice Clone) -- $30 Mortal Kombat 2 Blu-ray preorders are now live Preorder at Walmart While its theatrical run isn't over yet, you can also preorder the upcoming Blu-ray release of Mortal Kombat 2. Special features and other bonuses haven't been revealed at the time of writing, but like its predecessor, there'll likely be a healthy amount of bonus materials detailing how the movie and its gory fight scenes were made. As usual, you'll be able to pick this up on 4K , standard Blu-ray , and DVD . Walmart has the best-looking version , as its exclusive steelbook 4K edition features the menacing glare of the movie villain Shao Khan on the front, the steely gaze of princess Kitana on the rear, and the showdown between Liu Kang and Kung Lao on the inside.

articles.title: GameSpot is moving!
articles.description: Hey GameSpot community, We’ve got some big news to share: GameSpot is migrating to a new platform. After 13 years on this platform – a custom built CMS that served us well across different owners and different eras of web publishing – we’re moving to WordPress VIP, a best-in-class solution that will allow us to shift our energy from maintaining a website to innovating on one. What this means for you Your GameSpot account will move over to the new site, same email, same password. There’s no re-registration, no password reset emails, it just works. What isn’t coming yet are the old site’s user-generated components. We took a long look at how you all used the site over the years and, due to lack of attention and increased competition, it’s clear that these tools didn’t meet your needs anymore. To be clear, we think there’s even more room for innovation here, and this migration is intended to help us capture it, and that starts with deciding where we can and can’t win. We’re rolling this out gradually rather than flipping a switch overnight. For a window of time, traffic will be split between the new site and this one — some readers will land on the new experience, while others will stay here while we monitor things and iron out any wrinkles. If you get the new site, but want to go back and peruse any of your contributions on the old site, those addresses will continue to direct to the old site during this window. That’s https://gamespot.com/login/ , gamespot.com/forums/ , gamespot.com/pm/inbox/ (private messages), and gamespot.com/profiles/ . If you’re still on the old site, congrats! You’ll have some time to kick around these virtual hallways before we send it off. What’s coming with you First, there’s obviously a new look and feel. You may recognize this branding work from our videos and social channels, but it hasn’t made its way to the site until now. Page layouts have been modernized, the actual text is easier to read, and everything from images to ads will load faster. This is a top-to-bottom overhaul of the site that will serve as a foundation for the coming years and decades of GameSpot, so we can continue serving as your trusted guide to all things video games, from the games themselves to the culture around them. And one last thing here: while comments aren’t enabled at launch, we will be migrating those over in the near future once we catch our breath. Feedback If you hit a bug or something feels off on the new site, please let us know! You can reach us here . But this migration isn’t just a backend project — it’s a chance to rethink what GameSpot should be in 2026 and beyond. So we’re asking: What do you want from GameSpot?
